Built for AMD AM5 CPUs, it comes with a total of 13 USBs at the rear, including two USB-C ports rated for 40 Gbps. Four M.2 sockets are included for a whole array of SSDs, with two rated for PCIe 5.0 over four lanes with the 9800X3D installed. It also has fast networking support built-in, with 2.5G and Wi-Fi 7 support included, as well as the ability to install a whopping 256GB of DDR5 RAM over four DIMM slots, with speeds of up to 8,200 MT/s.This deal, unfortunately, doesn't stretch to the full 256GB (given sky-high RAM prices at the moment, that's wishful thinking), but you’re still getting a fast set of 32GB memory from Corsair. The Corsair Vengeance DDR5 RAM included here comes in a set of 2 x 16GB sticks, supporting speeds of 6,400 MT/s. It’s fast and includes ten-zone configurable RGB lighting on top, and it's a popular choice among gamers.As deals go, this 9800X3D combo deal with an Asus TUF Gaming motherboard and 32GB of DDR5 RAM for just $989.98 is one you're not going to want to miss out on. If you’re thinking about a new rig or an upgrade, this will get you most of the parts you’ll need to set yourself up for gaming at 4K, as long as you get a GPU to match. history.author: Ben Stockton
history.titleZh: Newegg史诗级套装：AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D搭配32GB疾速DDR5内存与华硕X870E电竞主板仅售990美元——狂省200美元使内存成本骤降至196美元，更免费附赠一体式水冷散热器与游戏大作
history.descriptionZh:
知名硬件零售商Newegg近日推出了一项极具吸引力的PC硬件套装优惠，将AMD旗舰游戏处理器、高速内存和高端主板组合销售，为玩家打造高性能4K游戏平台提供了高性价比的入门选择。

此次促销的核心套装包含三大件：AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D处理器、32GB Corsair Vengeance DDR5-6400内存以及Asus TUF Gaming X870E-Plus WiFi 7主板。套装原价1193.97美元，现价仅为989.98美元，相当于直接节省了203.99美元。此外，购买该套装还将免费获赠一款MSI MAG Coreliquid A13 240mm一体式水冷散热器和《红色沙漠》游戏。若单独购买处理器和主板就需793.98美元，因此套装中的32GB内存实际仅相当于196美元，远低于其399.99美元的常规零售价，性价比突出。

**核心技术解析：AMD 3D V-Cache的统治力**
这套配置的灵魂在于AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D处理器。它基于Zen 5架构，拥有8个核心，基础频率4.7 GHz，加速频率可达5.2 GHz。其最核心的创新与优势来自于AMD独有的3D V-Cache技术。该技术通过3D堆叠，为处理器额外增加了巨大的L3缓存，使得Ryzen 7 9800X3D的三级缓存总量达到了惊人的96MB。在游戏中，更大的缓存可以显著减少CPU访问延迟更高的系统内存（DRAM）的频率，从而直接、有效地提升游戏性能，带来更高且更稳定的帧率。正是凭借这项技术，AMD的X3D系列处理器长期以来统治着游戏CPU的性能排行榜，而9800X3D在近期才被其兄弟型号9850X3D略微超越，其游戏性能实力可见一斑。

**配套高端硬件，构建完整游戏平台**
与这颗顶级游戏CPU相匹配的是一块功能强大的高端主板——华硕TUF Gaming X870E-Plus WiFi 7。它专为AMD AM5平台设计，提供了丰富的扩展能力：包括13个后置USB接口（其中两个为40 Gbps的USB-C）、4个M.2插槽（其中两个在与9800X3D搭配时可支持PCIe 5.0 x4）。网络方面，它集成了2.5G有线网卡和最新的Wi-Fi 7无线支持。主板还拥有4个DDR5内存插槽，最高可支持256GB容量及8200 MT/s的速度。

套装中包含的Corsair Vengeance DDR5-6400内存为2x16GB套装，运行在6400 MT/s的速度上，并带有10区可配置RGB灯效，是游戏玩家的热门选择。虽然当前内存价格高企，套装未配备顶格的256GB内存，但32GB的容量对于绝大多数游戏和应用已完全足够。

**应用场景与购买建议**
该套装为用户构建一台全新的4K分辨率游戏电脑提供了核心基础。用户只需在此基础上额外选购显卡、机箱、电源和 peripherals 等部件即可组成完整系统。由于节省了超过200美元，玩家可以将预算更多地投入到RTX 4090或Radeon RX 7900 XTX等高端显卡上，以获得极致的4K游戏体验。

此类硬件捆绑优惠通常非常抢手，很快就会售罄。对于正计划组建新游戏主机或进行平台升级的用户而言，这是一个以合理价格获取顶级游戏CPU、高速内存和高质量主板的难得机会。如果用户希望寻找更多优惠，也可以关注相关的显卡、固态硬盘、显示器等专题 deals 页面。

Subscribe to our newsletter As time goes on without much relief in sight from these astronomical prices, we find ourselves relying more and more on bundle deals to get pricing back down to reasonable (for the times) price points. Case in point: we found this awesome Newegg bundle that includes MSI’s flagship X870E Godlike motherboard, 32GB of DDR5-6400 Corsair Vengeance RAM, and AMD’s Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU for only $1,624.98, saving almost $300 versus buying the parts separately.This combo starts off with one of the most frequently purchased current-generation CPUs in the Ryzen 7 9800X3D. This 8-core/16-thread Zen5-based processor has a base clock of 4.6 GHz and a max boost of 5.2 GHz, which is plenty of speed for any task. The 96MB of L3 cache on the X3D variant helps with gaming (among other things), making it one of the fastest gaming CPUs available. It is also a well-rounded performer and a good match for all but the most thread-heavy applications. ● Check out this deal at Newegg Also in the combo is Corsair Vengeance RGB 32GB (2x16GB) DDR5-6400 CL36 RAM, priced at $399.99 when bought alone. The black kit has a frosted RGB light bar that runs the length of the sticks and is sure to draw attention inside your case. Or, if RGB lighting isn’t your thing and you prefer a stealthy appearance, you can also disable it (on the motherboard, too). The DDR5-6400 speed lands on the faster side of AMD’s sweet spot and is a worthwhile, good-looking option for a 32GB kit. The SK Hynix ICs under the heatsink pair nicely with the X870E Apex and offer plenty of overclocking headroom. Buying 32GB of DDR5 with reasonable speeds for $101 is a heck of a deal. free msi mag coreliquid 240mm AIO and Crimson DesertSave $299.99AMD, MSI, Corsair | AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D, MSI MEG X870E Godlike, 32GB Corsair Vengeance DDR5-6400 RAM: was $1,924.97 now $1,624.98 at NeweggGet a great deal on this high-end bundle from Newegg that includes a flaghship motherboard in MSI X870E Godlike, 32GB of Corsair Vengeance RAM, and the Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU for only $1,624.98. You also get a free 240mm AIO and the Crimson Desert game.View Deal We've included a couple of charts that show how well the 9800X3D performs among its peers. As you can see, it's a beast in gaming, beat out only by the 9850X3D (and negligibly, really), and holds its own in multi-threaded workloads. Image 1 of 3(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future)(Image credit: Future) Last, Newegg includes a flagship motherboard on the MSI MEG X870E Godlike. This E-ATX motherboard we reviewed (and loved, earning 4.5 stars and ranking among our best motherboards) gives you the best of what the platform offers. You get a whopping seven M.2 sockets with the included XPander-Z card, a cool-looking Dynamic Dashboard LED that displays system information (or your own image), and a total of 15 USB ports on the rear IO (seven of which are Type-C, two of which are USB4/40 Gbps). I also believe it’s one of the better-looking original X870 flagships and a worthwhile centerpiece for your AMD-based system.If you’re in the market for a new AM5 build and need, or just want, all of the functionality (and bragging rights) that a flagship-class motherboard offers, this is one of the best bundles available for a high-end system on Newegg. They also throw in a free copy of Crimson Desert and an MSI MAG Coreliquid A13 230 Black AIO, potentially saving another $154.99. From the best-selling gaming processor in the Ryzen 7 9800X3D to the 32GB of Corsair Vengeance RAM that’s essentially $101 in this premium combo, you’ll want to jump on this deal before it’s too late. history.author: Joe Shields
history.titleZh: 以101美元购得32GB海盗船DDR5内存——新蛋组合套装还包含微星旗舰X870E Godlike主板与AMD锐龙9800X3D处理器，仅售1624.98美元。
history.descriptionZh:
在当前硬件价格持续高企的市场背景下，消费者愈发依赖捆绑促销来获得接近合理价位的顶级配置。Newegg平台近日推出了一款极具吸引力的高端组合套餐，原价1924.97美元，现仅售1624.98美元，直接节省近300美元。该套装核心包含三件旗舰级组件：AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D处理器、MSI MEG X870E Godlike主板以及32GB Corsair Vengeance DDR5-6400内存。此外，购买还附赠一款MSI MAG Coreliquid 240mm一体式水冷散热器和《赤色沙漠》游戏，潜在附加价值超过150美元。

**核心技术原理与创新点**
套装的核心是AMD Ryzen 7 9800X3D处理器。它基于Zen 5架构，拥有8核16线程，基础频率4.6 GHz，最大加速频率可达5.2 GHz。其最大的技术创新在于搭载了96MB的3D V-Cache高速缓存。这项技术通过垂直堆叠额外的L3缓存，显著降低了处理器核心访问数据的延迟，特别有利于提升对缓存敏感的应用程序性能，尤其是在游戏中表现卓越。

配套的MSI MEG X870E Godlike主板是AMD X870E平台的旗舰之作，采用E-ATX版型。其创新点包括：随附的XPander-Z扩展卡提供了多达七个M.2 SSD插槽；配备了动态仪表盘LED，可实时显示系统信息或自定义图像；后置I/O面板集成了多达15个USB接口，其中7个为Type-C，更有2个支持USB4/40 Gbps高速标准。这些设计为高端系统提供了极致的扩展能力和视觉个性。

内存方面，套装搭载了Corsair Vengeance RGB 32GB (2x16GB) DDR5-6400 CL36套条。其设计亮点在于覆盖整个内存条的磨砂RGB灯带，同时支持在主板中禁用灯光以实现低调外观。该内存采用了SK海力士芯片，与X870E平台搭配具有良好的超频潜力。

**性能参数与对比分析**
性能图表显示，Ryzen 7 9800X3D在游戏性能上表现堪称猛兽，仅略逊于更高阶的Ryzen 9 9850X3D，差距微乎其微。同时，它在多线程工作负载中也保持了强大的竞争力，是一款性能全面的处理器。DDR5-6400的频率正处于AMD AM5平台的性能甜点区，能够充分释放平台潜力。在本次捆绑销售中，这套32GB内存的等效价格仅约101美元，性价比极高。

**技术影响与应用场景**
此捆绑套装代表了当前AM5平台的顶级配置之一。Ryzen 7 9800X3D巨大的L3缓存使其成为追求极致帧率的游戏玩家的首选；强大的多线程性能也足以应对内容创作、软件开发等专业任务。MSI Godlike主板提供的极致连接性和扩展能力，满足了发烧友、高性能工作站用户对多高速存储设备和外围设备的需求。整套系统不仅性能强悍，其主板的光效设计、内存的RGB灯条以及附赠的水冷散热器也共同构建了出色的视觉体验，适合打造高性能游戏主机或炫酷的展示型系统。

总而言之，这套捆绑方案在显著降低总价的同时，汇集了在各自领域性能领先、设计出众的组件，并为用户提供了额外的赠品价值，为计划组建高端AM5平台的用户提供了一个难得的高性价比入手机会。

history.title: Silverstone IceMyst Pro 360 Pro Review: Designed for RAM overclocking
history.description: Silverstone’s IceMyst Pro 360 is a unique AIO that’s designed for RAM overclocking. This review is tested with AMD’s Ryzen 9 9950X3D CPU.
history.content: Introduction to Silverstone IceMyst Pro 360 ProSilverStone has been busy churning out coolers, power supplies, expansion cards, and other PC equipment since its founding in 2003. The company is perhaps best known for its computer cases, like the retro-inspired PC FLP02.A few years ago we tested the company’s unique IceMyst 360 AIO with Intel’s Core i7-13700K. Since then, the bar for AIOs has been raised – we’ve seen improvements in affordability and performance of high end AIOs, with the best liquid coolers becoming stronger and more competitive every year. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Today we’re looking at Silverstone’s IceMyst 360 Pro, which, like its predecessor, features the ability to add multiple VRM fans to provide direct cooling to specific motherboard components. This feature will be appreciated best by those who overclock their RAM, as DDR5 is more sensitive to temperature than older DDR4. Will this liquid cooler make our list of the best CPU coolers? You betcha! But let's take a look at the specifications and features of the cooler, then we’ll go over thermal and noise benchmarks so you can decide if this is the right AIO for your next build.Silverstone IceMyst Pro 360 Pro at Amazon for $189.99Cooler specificationsSwipe to scroll horizontallyCoolerSilverstone IceMyst Pro 360ColorsBlackMSRP$189.99LightingARGBWarranty3 yearsSocket CompatibilityIntel LGA 1851/1700/1200/115xRow 6 - Cell 0 AMD sTR5/TR4/AM5/AM4Radiator dimensions396 (L) x 120 (W) x 28mm (H)Maximum TDP (Our Testing)>250W with AMD’s Ryzen 9 9950X3DToday's best Silverstone IceMyst Pro 360 Pro deals $189.99View $209.08ViewWe check over 250 million products every day for the best pricesFeatures of the IceMyst Pro 360 AIO liquid cooler● Packaging (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The outside of the box is brown and blue, highlighting the cooler’s image on the top. As with most AIOs, the inner contents are protected with molded carboard and plastic coverings. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Included with the AIO are the following parts:● 120mm fansStay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThere’s more to an AIO than just the liquid pump and radiator. The fans included have a direct impact on noise levels and cooling performance. Silverstone includes three model CC12025H12S fans, which are 120mm in size and feature nine fan blades. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)To simplify cable management, these fans feature a quick-connect that integrates ARGB and PWM into a single cable. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)● 360mm radiator and liquid pump (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The radiator is that it includes an accessible refill port, which will come in useful if you ever need to refill the liquid coolant after the unit’s three-year warranty. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)This “Pro” version of the IceMyst 360 features a new pump with a three-phase, six-pole motor. Silverstone claims that this pump delivers twice the flow rate of its previous products. (Image credit: Silverstone)● A small tube of thermal paste (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)● Hardware ARGB Control BoxIncluded with the AIO is a hardware ARGB controller that lets you control and power the lighting without needing an available motherboard connection. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)● Mounting for modern AMD and Intel platforms, and even ThreadRipper Pro!Like most other liquid coolers, the Silverstone IceMyst Pro 360 supports modern AMD and Intel consumer platforms. It includes a very large copper CPU contact plate, because the cooler also includes mounting hardware for Threadripper Pro systems. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)● The killer feature: (Optional) IMF70 VRM/DDR5 fansThe feature that sets the IceMyst Pro 360 Pro apart from competitors is the ability to mount mulitple IMF70 fans on top of the CPU block. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)You can install as many or few fans as your case will fit, and you can move the fans to cover different spots on your motherboard as appropriate. For the purposes of this review, we’ve tested the AIO in multiple fan configurations above our TeamGroup T-Force Xtreem DDR5-7200 RAM. And we’ve run the ultimate RAM stress test, Karhu, to benchmark thermals and determine how effective these fans are. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)● AM4/AM5 InstallationThe installation of this liquid cooler is simple, but it doesn’t come with the fans pre-installed. So first you’ll want to secure the fans to the radiator, and then mount the radiator to your computer’s case. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Next, you’ll remove the default AM4/AM5 mechanism, and then place studs on the mounting holes. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)It’s time to apply the included thermal paste now. If you’re not sure how to do that, we have a handy thermal past guide. Once the paste is applied, simply place the pump block on top of the CPU and secure it with the included parts.With that step completed, you have the option to install an optional IMF70 VRM/DDR5 fan. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The last step is to connect the PWM and ARGB headers and power on your system. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)Real-world testing configuration – AMD AM5 platformThe 9950X3D setup that we were testing coolers with last year has suffered an early death, likely caused by electrically defective equipment. While we were waiting for the CPU to be replaced, we tested coolers with AMD’s Ryzen 9 9950X instead and observed some interesting findings.There are some differences in how the 9950X and 9950X3D CPUs are impacted by thermal events – while the heat output of the CCDs of AMD’s 9950X3D are relatively balanced, the 9950X I use has one CCD that runs much hotter than the other, with a difference of over 10 degrees Celsius in some scenarios, shown below. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)The benchmark results shared in these reviews may differ from others because I place an emphasis on having results that are comparable to real-world use.This means that I generally test CPU coolers inside of a closed desktop case, which increases cooling difficulty compared to other testing method. Many will test CPU coolers outside of a case, on an open test bench. Open benches have lowered ambient temperatures, which in turn makes weak coolers appear stronger than they really are. Some publications have used generic thermal plates to test cooling solutions. I reject both of these methods because they don’t accurately reflect the real-world PC cooler conditions.Swipe to scroll horizontallyCPUAMD Ryzen 9 9950X3DGPUMSI Ventus 3X RTX 4070Ti SuperRAMTeamGroup Diamond Rose T-Force Xtreem DDR5-7200MotherboardMSI X870E Carbon WifiCaseTryx FLOVA F50Our latest testing setup uses the FLOVA F50 computer case from Tryx. (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)This case features a unique “crossflow” fan that pulls air from the side, which the company claims is more effective than traditional intake fans. history.author: Albert Thomas
history.titleZh: Silverstone IceMyst Pro 360 Pro评测：专为内存超频设计
history.descriptionZh:
银欣（SilverStone）作为2003年成立的PC硬件品牌，长期致力于散热器、电源、扩展卡等设备研发，其复古风格的电脑机箱（如PC FLP02）尤为知名。继数年前推出独特的IceMyst 360一体式水冷散热器（AIO）后，银欣如今发布了升级版IceMyst Pro 360 Pro。在高端AIO市场竞争日趋激烈、性能与性价比同步提升的背景下，这款新品最大的亮点在于延续并强化了其标志性的可扩展设计：支持加装多个VRM风扇，为主板特定组件（尤其是内存和供电模块）提供定向主动散热。该功能对内存超频用户意义重大，因为DDR5内存对温度比DDR4更敏感。目前该散热器在亚马逊售价189.99美元。

**核心技术原理与创新点**
IceMyst Pro 360 Pro的核心创新在于其模块化散热设计。除了常规的360mm冷排和冷头，它允许用户在CPU冷头上方灵活安装多个选配的IMF70小型风扇（尺寸70mm），直接对主板VRM供电区域和DDR5内存进行强制风冷。用户可根据机箱空间和主板布局决定安装风扇的数量与位置，从而实现定制化散热。
此外，该散热器在泵体技术上进行了升级：采用了新型三相六极电机水泵，银欣宣称其流速达到前代产品的两倍。冷排上还设计了可重复注液口，便于用户在三年质保期后维护冷却液，延长产品寿命。附件中包含一个独立的ARGB灯光控制盒，即使主板没有可用接口也能实现对灯效的管控。兼容性方面，它支持英特尔LGA 1851/1700/1200/115x和AMD sTR5/TR4/AM5/AM4平台，甚至包括Threadripper Pro系统，为此配备了大尺寸铜质底座。

**性能测试与对比分析**
本次评测采用了贴近真实使用场景的测试方法：所有测试均在封闭式机箱（Tryx FLOVA F50）内进行，而非开放的测试平台，这更能反映散热器在实战环境中的性能。测试平台采用AMD Ryzen 9 9950X处理器（注：原计划使用的9950X3D因故障送修）、MSI X870E Carbon Wifi主板和TeamGroup T-Force Xtreem DDR5-7200内存。
测试中特别对比了加装与不加装IMF70 VRM风扇两种情况下的散热效果。在运行高负载内存压力测试（Karhu）时，为内存增加了两个IMF70风扇进行主动散热。结果显示，在内存区域无主动风扇时，内存温度在测试中显著上升；而加装两个IMF70风扇后，内存温度得到有效抑制，降温效果明显，证明了该设计对DDR5高温控制的有效性。
针对CPU散热，该散热器在压制9950X时表现稳健。测试人员特别指出，手中的9950X样品存在CCD核心温差较大的情况（差距超10°C），这在评估散热均匀性时是一个现实考量。散热器的整体散热能力（TDP）标称可支持超过250W，足以应对高端处理器。

**技术影响与应用场景**
IceMyst Pro 360 Pro的差异化设计直接瞄准了高端超频玩家、工作站用户和追求极致散热的发烧友。其可扩展的VRM/DDR5风扇方案，在AIO市场中提供了通常只有复杂分体水冷或额外加装风扇才能实现的精准主板组件散热能力，简化了用户的优化步骤。易维护的注液口设计也提升了产品的长期可用性价值。
总体而言，银欣IceMyst Pro 360 Pro通过其模块化附加风扇系统、升级的高流速水泵和广泛的平台兼容性，在高端AIO市场中构建了独特的竞争力。对于计划组建高性能、高稳定性平台，特别是打算对DDR5内存和CPU进行超频的用户，它提供了一个省心且高效的集成散热解决方案。其189.99美元的定价使其在高端竞品中具备一定的性价比优势。

history.title: Newbie overclocker destroys $5,000 RTX 5090 Lightning Z GPU they used 'to learn how to solder' — practicing newfound hobby goes wrong in the most expensive way imaginable
history.description: An MSI GeForce RTX 5090 Lightning Z owner sent their $5,000 GPU for repair after damaging it while "practicing" soldering a resistor on the board.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 10 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Subscribe to our newsletter A newbie overclocker apparently learned how to solder on their expensive RTX 5090 GPU and ended up damaging it while practicing their newfound skill. According to NorthridgeFix, which received the broken graphics card for repair, the unit came with this note: “I wanted to learn how to solder tiny 0402 resistors and was practicing on my GPU. I ended up ripping one of the pads and now the GPU will not post. If possible, I’d like to repair the pad/trace and re-align the resistors.” One possible reason for this mod is that the owner of the graphics card was trying to install the custom 2500W XOC BIOS, which MSI has limited to a select few elite overclockers, and requires physically modifying the card. Rare $5,000 GPU Destroyed… Only 1,300 Exist - YouTube Watch On MSI released the RTX 5090 Lightning Z GPU at CES 2026 to great fanfare, with the GPU beating several world records even before it launched. These graphics cards use a specialized BIOS that unlocks their capabilities. The firmware was leaked online and posted by TechPowerUp, but the company requested that it be taken down. MSI didn’t just stop at limiting its spread, though — it turns out that the company made it impossible to flash its 2500W XOC BIOS to retail MSI GPUs. VideoCardz says that one workaround to this restriction is to add an extra resistor to the $5,000 GPU.However, it seems that this mod is easier said than done, with the customer stating that they ended up ripping up one of the pads and misaligning the resistor during their botched operation. Alex, the host of the NorthridgeFix video, was incredulous when they found the damaged part, which was right next to the GPU core and connected directly to it. “The customer was trying to practice on the tightest spot on the board,” Alex, the host of the NorthridgeFix video, said. “Why go up the ladder one step at a time? Why not go a hundred steps at a time? That’s lot faster.”Article continues below You may like $5,000 RTX 5090 Lightning Z gets killed in extreme overclocking attempt, thermal shock cracks the GPU core MSI RTX 5090 Lightning Z GPUs listed on eBay for almost $27,000 Flabbergasted GPU repair wizard highlights dangers of liquid metal after leak kills entire RTX 5070 Ti Even seasoned veterans in the overclocking industry can get carried away and destroy their expensive components in their quest to squeeze every drop of performance out of them. This means that beginners in the overclocking scene are much more likely to do damage to their parts. You also need a lot of cash to get into this passion, as manufacturers often reserve high-end overclocking for their most expensive components. Still, it seems that people who are passionate enough about this are willing to risk thousands of dollars, and even limited-edition models with only 1,300 units in the wild.After an involved repair process, NorthridgeFix seems to have replaced the damaged parts, and everything seemed to be in order when it was run through the voltage tester. Unfortunately, we did not see the GPU turn on the video because they were only set up with a single 12VHPWR cable, whereas the GPU required two. They tried running it with their available cable, but the graphics card refused to power on, even though Alex is confident in his repair. As of now, we can only hold our breath and wait for an update from NorthridgeFix if it was successfully able to resurrect this limited-edition graphics card from the dead. history.author: Jowi Morales
history.titleZh: 新晋超频爱好者因练习焊接损毁价值五千美元的RTX 5090 Lightning Z显卡——昂贵兴趣初尝试付出惨痛代价
history.descriptionZh:
一名超频新手在价值昂贵的RTX 5090 Lightning Z显卡上练习焊接技术，导致这款全球仅1300片的限量版显卡严重受损。根据维修机构NorthridgeFix收到的故障卡及附言，用户自称“想学习焊接微小的0402电阻，于是在自己的GPU上练习”，结果不慎扯掉了一个焊盘并导致电阻错位，致使显卡无法启动。

**技术背景与事故根源**
此次事故与MSI为精英超频者特制的2500W极限超频（XOC）BIOS直接相关。该BIOS能彻底释放显卡性能，曾在CES 2026发布前创下多项世界纪录。尽管BIOS文件曾遭泄露，但MSI通过在零售版显卡中设置硬件锁，使普通用户无法直接刷入。据VideoCardz透露，破解此限制需在显卡特定位置增焊电阻——这正是用户尝试的改装操作。

**维修难度与行业反思**
维修视频主持人Alex发现，损坏的焊盘位于GPU核心旁的关键电路区域，直连核心芯片。他震惊地表示：“用户竟选择在主板上最精密的区域练习焊接，这就像不循序渐进，而试图一步登天。” 此次事件折射出硬核超频领域的风险：即使经验丰富的超频者也常因追求极致性能而损坏硬件，新手更易操作失误。该领域入门门槛极高，厂商通常仅在最昂贵的限量组件上开放超频潜力，而爱好者们仍愿为此承担数千美元的风险。

**维修进展与悬而未决**
经过复杂维修，NorthridgeFix已更换受损元件，电压测试显示电路恢复正常。但由于测试时仅连接单根12VHPWR供电线（该显卡需双线供电），未能成功点亮显卡。维修人员对修复结果保持信心，但最终能否让这片限量显卡“起死回生”，仍需等待后续测试报告。

此事件也呼应了近期高端显卡改装风险：此前曾有RTX 5090 Lightning Z因液金泄漏导致核心破裂，以及同型号显卡在二手市场被炒至27000美元的现象。这些案例共同揭示了极致超频文化背后高昂的技术门槛与财务风险。

history.title: Ambitious hacker reduces worst-case memory latency by up to 93%, but with severe downsides — 1960s bottleneck overcome by hedging memory accesses to avoid running into DRAM refresh stalls
history.description: The clever software trick works on both x86 and Arm to radically reduce worst-case memory latency, but it has severe limitations, too.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Tom's Hardware Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware Newsletter Get Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox. Contact me with news and offers from other Future brands Receive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors By submitting your information you agree to the Terms & Conditions and Privacy Policy and are aged 16 or over. You are now subscribed Your newsletter sign-up was successful An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter A method devised by YouTuber, Googler, and security researcher LaurieWired could have huge implications for a very few specific use cases and workloads that are highly sensitive to "tail latency," or near-worst-case memory access latency. The project is called TailSlayer, and fundamentally, leverages hedging memory accesses to avoid running into DRAM refresh stalls.Without getting fully into the weeds, the type of memory we all use, DRAM, has one serious downside: it has to be refreshed constantly. The cells where DRAM stores its data are fundamentally tiny capacitors, and they are highly leaky by design, so we have to continually top up the charge in them to make sure that they retain their data. This is known as DRAM Refresh. The refresh cycle happens at a rate that varies widely depending on the system and type of DRAM in question, but generally it's going to happen at an interval that is measured in microseconds, meaning that your memory is refreshing hundreds of times in the time it takes you to blink. The takeaway here is mostly that modern computers operate at absurd speeds. (Image credit: LaurieWired)Now, DRAM refreshes aren't performed synchronously with memory accesses. Because of that, it's entirely possible that your system may try to access memory that's currently being refreshed. If that happens, the request simply stalls until the refresh cycle is finished. This can cause a stall of hundreds of nanoseconds, which isn't a long time in absolute terms, but at the speeds of modern chips, even a 200-nanosecond stall can be a thousand cycles where a CPU core isn't getting any work done.Article continues below You may like Deepseek research touts memory breakthrough, decoupling compute power and RAM pools to bypass GPU constraints AI memory crunch forces DRAM market into 'hourly pricing' model, report claims Samsung, SK Hynix, Micron team up to block memory hoarding This isn't a major problem for most use cases because we have all kinds of strategies in place to deal with stalls like this; this problem has been known since the 1960s when DRAM was invented, so naturally, hundreds of very smart people have put their genius to work developing workarounds. However, certain very specific workloads are extremely sensitive to non-deterministic memory latency, and these DRAM refresh cycle stalls are a major source of exactly that kind of behavior.So what can you do? LaurieWired decided to tackle this problem for reasons she never fully elucidates but which probably boil down to "it was interesting," given her other work. Her initial ideas involve attempting to predict DRAM refreshes and synchronize around them, but that's completely impossible for several reasons she goes over in her video. Her next idea was parallelism, but she was stymied by CPU cache and reorder buffers—CPU features that largely obviate the issue in the first place. Using multiple CPU cores allows the technique to work, as it prevents multiple types of stalls. (Image credit: LaurieWired)Her breakthrough was when she realized she didn't necessarily have to do all of this on one CPU core. In the end, what she did was elect to fully duplicate the working set across memory addressing boundaries, ensuring that each copy resides on a different physical memory channel with independent timing behavior, and then run her operations simultaneously on two different CPU cores with each accessing a different memory channel. Then, she could simply let them race to finish and take the result of the one that finishes first; if one core hits a DRAM refresh interval, the likelihood that the other core also does is pretty low. This method allowed her to reduce the tail latency of DRAM accesses on her consumer Ryzen desktop system by more than half, which is huge.By renting server time on Amazon AWS instances, she was able to test on high-end AMD, Intel, and Arm server hardware. She managed to achieve far greater results on these machines for a few reasons: they have slower CPU clock rates and slower memory, and they also have more conservative memory timings, that mean stalls are even worse for performance. The real difference is in the number of available memory channels, though. An EPYC Turin processor has some twelve memory channels, and by executing her strategy there, hedging across all twelve channels, she was able to cut near-worst-case memory latency (tail latency) by a staggering 89%.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors You'll want to expand this image, which shows the frankly shocking memory latency improvements achieved using TailSlayer on a variety of systems. (Image credit: LaurieWired)The clever hacker achieved even better results on Intel and Arm hardware. On Intel Xeon processors from the Sapphire Rapids and Diamond Rapids families, she managed to achieve gains as high as 93.3%, or in other words, she slashed p99.99 memory latency from 1697ns all the way down to 113ns. Considering the lowest value on the chart is around 105ns, that means the Xeon managed to achieve unbelievably deterministic memory latency.I've said a few times that "certain workloads" benefit from TailSlayer. The most obvious place where determinism in memory latency is absolutely critical is in the slightly absurd world of high-frequency trading (HFT). If you're not familiar, imagine a bunch of hyper-caffeinated algorithms in a cage match where the winner is whoever can buy or sell a stock a few microseconds before everyone else. HFT firms spend obscene amounts of money on servers co-located right next to the exchange's matching engine, shaving off nanoseconds with custom hardware, microwave links instead of fiber optics, and code so obsessively optimized it would make a demoscene guru blush.These systems operate with such tight tolerances that if a memory access runs into a DRAM refresh cycle, the opportunity is likely missed, potentially costing the firm millions of dollars. Because of that, the HFT world is the most obvious place where technology like this could be deployed, and it's almost one of the only places where it makes sense. There are other workloads that benefit from eliminating DRAM refresh stalls, sure; high-QPS microservices, matching engines, real-time ranking structures, anything using concurrent queues, and even potentially simulators or game servers, particularly those operating with a high level of precision. (Image credit: LaurieWired)The problem with using TailSlayer for many of these workloads will have already become apparent to many of you reading this, and it's primarily that Laurie's method requires fully duplicating the working set of the application for each memory channel you're hedging across. You're trading memory capacity and CPU cores for latency determinism, as this effectively multiplies memory requirements for any given application by a factor of the number of hedges you're willing to make. For some tasks—like, again, HFT—the actual memory requirements are quite modest, and so accepting a twelve-fold increase in memory usage in exchange for a 15× drop in p99.99 memory latency makes sense. For most workloads, it doesn't. Your RAM Has a 60 Year Old Design Flaw. I Bypassed It. - YouTube Watch On Of course, LaurieWired acknowledges this in her 54-minute video talking about the technique. In general, while she's (understandably!) quite pleased to have come up with TailSlayer, she's also quite frank about its relatively limited utility. Her video goes into significant depth about the research she had to do, including reverse-engineering undocumented memory scrambling behavior as well as devising a way to make the method work on Amazon's Graviton Arm-based CPUs, since they don't expose the same level of hardware counters that x86-64 CPUs do. It's highly recommended to watch if you're interested in the topic. Alternatively, you can head over to her GitHub repository to check out the demo code for yourself. history.author: Zak Killian
history.titleZh: 雄心勃勃的黑客将最坏情况下的内存延迟降低达93%，但代价沉重——通过交替式内存访问策略突破1960年代遗留瓶颈，成功规避DRAM刷新停顿
history.descriptionZh:
近日，一位来自谷歌的YouTuber兼安全研究员LaurieWired提出了一项名为“TailSlayer”的创新方法，旨在解决DRAM（动态随机存取存储器）刷新周期引发的“尾延迟”（tail latency）问题，即内存访问接近最坏情况下的延迟。这项技术对于对延迟高度敏感、要求极致确定性的特定应用场景，如高频交易（HFT），可能具有重大意义。

**背景与核心问题**
现代计算机中普遍使用的DRAM，其存储单元本质上是微小的电容器，电荷会自然泄露。因此，必须定期（通常以微秒为间隔）进行“刷新”（Refresh）以维持数据。关键问题在于，内存刷新操作与处理器的内存访问请求是异步进行的。当CPU试图访问正在刷新的内存行时，该请求将被阻塞，直到刷新完成。这种阻塞可能持续数百纳秒。虽然绝对时间不长，但对于以吉赫兹频率运行的现代CPU而言，这意味着可能浪费上千个时钟周期，导致核心空转。尽管自DRAM诞生以来，业界已发展出多种技术来缓解此类问题，但对于某些对非确定性延迟极其敏感的工作负载，DRAM刷新引起的延迟波动仍是一个主要痛点。

**TailSlayer 的核心原理与创新**
LaurieWired的研究摒弃了试图预测或同步DRAM刷新周期的传统思路，因为这些方法在实践中难以实现。她的突破性想法是采用“对冲”（hedging）和并行策略。具体而言，该方法的核心步骤如下：
1.  **数据完全复制**：将应用程序的工作集（working set）完整复制多份，并确保每个副本位于不同的物理内存通道上。每个内存通道具有独立的时序行为。
2.  **并行访问与结果竞速**：在不同的CPU核心上，同时发起对各自对应内存通道副本的相同操作。然后，简单地让这些操作“竞速”完成，并取最先返回的那个结果。
其原理基于一个关键观察：不同内存通道的刷新周期并不同步。因此，当一个核心的访问恰逢某个通道刷新而受阻时，另一个核心访问另一通道时恰好也遭遇刷新的概率非常低。通过这种“对冲”机制，系统能以额外的内存带宽和CPU核心资源为代价，极大地降低最坏情况下的访问延迟（尾延迟）。

**性能表现与对比数据**
LaurieWired首先在她的消费级AMD Ryzen台式机系统上进行了测试，成功将DRAM访问的尾延迟降低了一半以上。
随后，通过在亚马逊AWS云服务器上租用高端硬件进行更广泛测试，结果更为显著：
-   **AMD EPYC（霄龙）平台**：基于拥有12个内存通道的EPYC Turin处理器，通过对冲所有12个通道，她将接近最坏情况的内存延迟（尾延迟）惊人地降低了89%。
-   **Intel Xeon（至强）平台**：在Sapphire Rapids和Diamond Rapids系列的Xeon处理器上，取得了高达93.3%的增益。具体而言，将p99.99延迟（99.99%请求的最大延迟）从1697纳秒大幅削减至113纳秒，几乎达到了接近理论下限的确定性延迟水平。
-   **Arm平台**：在亚马逊的Graviton（基于Arm架构）CPU上也实现了优异的延迟降低效果，这需要克服硬件计数器暴露不足等额外挑战。

**技术影响与应用场景**
这项技术最直接、最理想的应用领域是**高频交易（HFT）**。HFT系统争夺微秒乃至纳秒级的优势，一次意外的内存刷新延迟就可能导致交易机会错失，造成巨大财务损失。因此，即使需要成倍增加内存使用量，换取延迟的极端确定性和数量级的降低，对HFT而言也是完全值得的交易。
除了HFT，其他可能受益的负载还包括：
-   高查询率（QPS）的微服务
-   匹配引擎（如交易所、广告竞价）
-   实时排名系统
-   使用并发队列的任何应用
-   高精度模拟器或游戏服务器

**局限性与权衡**
TailSlayer技术的主要局限性在于其资源开销。它需要为每一个用于对冲的内存通道完整复制工作集，这意味着**内存消耗将随对冲通道数成倍增长**。例如，在12通道系统上进行完全对冲，内存占用将变为原来的12倍。同时，还需要消耗额外的CPU核心来执行并行访问。
因此，该技术是一种典型的 **“以空间换时间”和“以核心资源换确定性”** 的策略。对于像HFT这样内存需求相对较小但延迟容忍度极低的场景，这种权衡是合理的。但对于大多数通用计算任务，内存容量和核心资源更为宝贵，此技术的适用性有限。
LaurieWired本人也在其详细介绍该技术的视频中坦率地指出了其应用范围相对特定。她的工作不仅包括构思算法，还涉及逆向工程未公开的内存扰码行为以及适配不同硬件架构，体现了深厚的技术探索精神。相关演示代码已在其GitHub仓库中开源。

history.title: Intel Arc GPUs can finally boot up and play 'Crimson Desert' — but you'll probably want to wait for official support
history.description: Intel's latest GPU driver is capable of booting Crimson Desert and can reportedly run at nearly 60 FPS at ultra settings on an Arc B580. But the game suffers from occasional crashing and visual artifacts.

Subscribe to our newsletter Intel Arc GPU owners can finally play Crimson Desert on their GPUs two weeks after the developers initially stated Arc GPUs would not be supported in-game. On the Intel Arc subreddit, gamers have found that Intel's latest driver 32.0.101.8629 can now technically run the game without crashing to the desktop for at least some users.Go deeper with TH Premium: GPUs (Image credit: Noctua)Desktop RoadmapEnterprise RoadmapRubin in-depthThe Stout Owl: The ultimate Noctua G2 PCGamers who have successfully booted the game on the subreddit report good performance with the new driver. One user claims they achieved nearly 60 FPS at 1440p ultra settings with an Arc B580, and another achieved playable performance on an Arc Pro B50 at 1080p high quality settings with ray tracing turned on.Crimson desert works with the new driver from r/IntelArcHowever, game support is far from perfect; game crashing still occurs with some Arc owners, and turning on FSR also causes the game to crash. Visual artifacts are also prevalent, especially on characters' faces. These problems explain why Crimson Desert support is not stated in the driver's patch notes. The only update Intel added to the patch notes is game-ready support for its Arc Pro B70 and Arc Pro B65 discrete GPUs.Article continues below You may like Intel has reached out to Crimson Desert developer “many times” to support Arc GPUs Crimson Desert offers no support for Intel GPUs Crimson Desert devs apologize for ‘confusion’ over Intel GPU FAQ — backtracks over prior dismissive language regarding Arc graphics support Regardless, the game is at least playable for a minority of Arc GPU owners. Before the update, the game would not boot up for anyone with an Arc GPU and would show an incompatibility error message.Back in late March, the development team behind Crimson Desert announced Arc GPU support would not be added to the game and even went so far as to recommend Arc GPU owners refund their graphics cards. Intel punched back at the devs, revealing to Tom's Hardware that it reached out to the team "many times" to provide assistance to make the game compatible with Intel GPUs.This back-and-forth led the developers to backtrack on their statement and begin working on compatibility and optimizations for Intel Arc GPUs. Official support is hopefully coming soon, but in the meantime, if you are an Arc owner, you can at least try to boot the game with Intel's latest driver if you don't want to wait for official support. history.titleZh: 英特尔锐炫显卡终可启动运行《赤色沙漠》——或许还应等待官方支持
history.descriptionZh:
经过近两个月的拉锯与合作，英特尔Arc显卡用户终于能够在他们的GPU上运行热门游戏《赤色沙漠》（Crimson Desert）了，尽管目前的体验尚不完美。

**背景与事件经过**
今年3月底，《赤色沙漠》的开发团队曾公开宣布，该游戏将不会添加对英特尔Arc GPU的支持，甚至建议Arc显卡用户考虑退款。这一声明引发了轩然大波。英特尔随即向媒体透露，其团队已“多次”主动联系游戏开发商，愿意提供技术支持以使游戏兼容英特尔显卡。这场公开的“交锋”最终促使游戏开发商改变了最初的态度，撤回了之前的声明，并开始着手为英特尔Arc GPU进行兼容性适配和优化工作。

**当前进展与核心技术原理**
目前，尽管官方对Arc GPU的完全支持尚未正式上线，但转机已经出现。在英特尔Arc显卡的Reddit社区中，用户发现，安装英特尔最新的**32.0.101.8629版本显卡驱动**后，部分用户已经可以成功启动并运行《赤色沙漠》，而不再像之前那样直接弹出不兼容的错误提示或崩溃到桌面。这表明，英特尔通过持续的驱动程序更新，已经初步解决了游戏引擎与Arc GPU架构之间的底层兼容性障碍。驱动程序作为硬件与游戏软件之间的翻译层，其优化是让新硬件支持老游戏或让新游戏适配新硬件的关键。

**性能表现与对比数据**
根据社区用户的反馈，在新的驱动下，游戏性能表现令人鼓舞。有用户报告称，使用**Arc B580显卡**在**1440p分辨率、超高画质设置**下，能够获得接近**60 FPS**的帧率。另一名使用**Arc Pro A50显卡**的用户则表示，在开启光线追踪的**1080p高画质**设置下，游戏性能已达到“可玩”水平。这些数据表明，一旦驱动优化到位，英特尔Arc显卡完全有能力流畅运行这款对硬件要求较高的新游戏。

**现存问题与技术影响分析**
然而，当前的支持状态远非完美，这也解释了为何此次更新并未被正式写入该版驱动的更新说明中。主要存在三大问题：
1.  **稳定性问题**：游戏崩溃仍然在一些Arc用户中发生。
2.  **功能兼容性问题**：开启AMD的FSR（FidelityFX Super Resolution）超分辨率技术会导致游戏崩溃，这可能是由于游戏内FSR的实现与Arc驱动存在冲突。
3.  **视觉瑕疵（Artifacts）**：游戏中普遍存在画面异常，尤其是在角色面部，出现纹理错误或显示异常。

这些问题的存在，凸显了游戏开发商与硬件厂商深度协作的必要性。驱动层面的初步兼容可以“点亮”游戏，但要获得稳定、无瑕疵的体验，仍需游戏开发者在代码层面进行针对性的优化和测试。此次事件也反映了英特尔在独立显卡市场生态建设上的积极姿态，即通过主动的技术支持和快速的驱动响应来争取开发者和用户，逐步改善其GPU的游戏兼容性声誉。

**应用场景与用户建议**
对于广大英特尔Arc显卡用户而言，这是一个积极的信号。如果你已经购买了《赤色沙漠》并拥有Arc显卡，现在可以尝试更新至最新的32.0.101.8629驱动程序来启动游戏。不过，需要做好可能遇到上述崩溃或画面问题的心理准备。对于更追求稳定体验的玩家，或许可以继续等待游戏开发商发布正式优化补丁。无论如何，从“完全无法运行”到“部分可玩”，这标志着英特尔Arc显卡在扩大其游戏兼容性库方面迈出了切实的一步。

Subscribe to our newsletter With production of Core Ultra 300 'Panther Lake' and Xeon 6+ 'Clearwater Forest' ramping at Intel's fab in Arizona, Intel is seemingly on the right track to recovery in the coming years. While it's not out of the woods yet, Intel's market capitalization on Thursday was its highest in over 25 years, or since the dot-com boom went bust, according to CompaniesMarketCap.com.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowIntel's market capitalization increased to $305 billion on Thursday, up 3.5X from mid-April 2025, and up 2.8X from August 20, 2025, when the U.S. government announced plans to acquire a 10% stake in Intel. The company is now the world's 48th most valuable company by market capitalization. While it trails ASML, AMD, Applied Materials, Lam Research, and other semiconductor peers, it is well ahead of KLA, IBM, Siemens, and Texas Instruments. (Image credit: Intel)The sharp increase follows Intel's announcement of Google's commitment to use Xeon processors for years to come, which emphasizes relevance of Intel's CPUs; the TeraFab partnership, which gives a nod to Intel's capabilities in design, produce, and package high-performance processors at scale; and a host of various AI-related narratives meant to demonstrate applicability of the company's products in the rapidly growing artificial intelligence sector.Article continues below You may like Intel shares down 13% as company only manages to shrink losses in latest earnings, demand to outpace 2026 supply Intel Q4 earnings reveal rocky path to recovery following weakest full-year revenue since 2010 Global semiconductor foundry market hit a record $320 billion in 2025 as TSMC pulled further ahead Intel's peak market capitalization was $502.71 billion at the height of the dot-com cycle in August 2000, when the company dominated both PCs and web servers. In today's dollars, that would be around $1.0 trillion. Formally, Intel's current capitalization of $305.25 billion is higher than its $219.1 billion capitalization in late 2003 when the company took the rapidly growing laptop market by storm with its Centrino platform and re-accelerated its growth.Nonetheless, $305 billion is higher than Intel's peaks in recent years: $257.23 billion in mid-2018 due to Intel's dominance in the growing data center sector $273.43 billion in early 2020 due to the cloud computing boom, and $262.87 billion in April 2021 due to the PC market and cloud computing growth amid the pandemic. That said, Intel is indeed going in the right direction, at least if you ask investors.Still, Intel's valuation history reflects a transition from a dominating CPU vendor commanding PCs and servers to a mature, execution-sensitive semiconductor company. Intel's market capitalization today is driven more by its strategic narratives associated with AI, foundry, and process technology ambitions, and mid-term product roadmaps than by its earnings. The recent climb toward ~$300 billion indicates renewed investor optimism, but unlike the 2000 peak, this optimism is based on expectations of a turnaround rather than on the already achieved dominance and expectations for skyrocketing sales. history.titleZh: 得益于与马斯克旗下TeraFab的合作，英特尔市值创25年新高——借CPU、AI与代工业务东风突破3000亿美元
history.descriptionZh:
近期，英特尔公司市值飙升至3050亿美元，创下自2000年互联网泡沫破灭以来超过25年的新高，标志着这家芯片巨头在经历了多年挑战后，正展现出强劲的复苏势头。这一增长源于多重战略利好消息的推动，包括下一代酷睿Ultra 300“Panther Lake”和至强6+“Clearwater Forest”处理器在亚利桑那州工厂的量产推进、谷歌承诺未来多年将继续采用至强处理器，以及TeraFab合作项目对英特尔大规模设计、制造和封装高性能处理器能力的认可。这些进展共同强化了英特尔在快速增长的AI领域的产品适用性及其长期技术路线图的吸引力。

从数据看，英特尔当前3050亿美元的市值较2025年4月中旬增长了约3.5倍，较2025年8月20日美国政府宣布计划收购其10%股份时增长了2.8倍。尽管这一数字仍低于其在2000年8月互联网鼎盛时期创下的约5027亿美元的历史峰值（经通胀调整后约合1万亿美元），但已显著超越了近年来的几个高点：2018年因数据中心市场主导地位达到的2572亿美元、2020年初云计算热潮下的2734亿美元，以及2021年4月疫情推动PC与云计算增长时的2629亿美元。当前市值也正式超过了2003年末其凭借迅驰平台席卷笔记本市场、重启增长周期时的2191亿美元。

与同业对比，英特尔目前是全球市值第48大公司，在半导体领域虽落后于阿斯麦、AMD、应用材料和拉姆研究等公司，但已领先于科磊、IBM、西门子和德州仪器。这一市值表现反映了一个深刻的战略转型：英特尔正从一个主导PC和服务器的CPU供应商，转变为一个更加成熟、执行力至上的综合半导体公司。当前市值的驱动因素，更多是基于其在人工智能、晶圆代工业务和先进制程技术上的战略叙事与中期产品规划，而非当前的盈利水平。

此次市值逼近3000亿美元，表明了投资者对英特尔扭亏为盈、重获竞争力的 renewed optimism。然而，与2000年基于已实现的垄断地位和销售暴涨预期的乐观不同，当前的乐观情绪更侧重于对未来转型成功的预期。总体而言，尽管前路依然充满挑战，但市场信号显示，英特尔至少在投资者眼中，正走在正确的复兴轨道上。
